DISCOVERY 302, DISCOVERY 302, FURTHER HEARING ON PLAINTIFF RONALD VAN SIEGMAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANTS PERSON MOST QUALIFIED/PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE (SPECIALLY SET AT 1:30 PM BEFORE JUDGE PRO TEM ADRIENNE ROGERS) THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) DEFENDANT MUST PRODUCE A PERSON MOST QUALIFIED TO TESTIFY ON ITS BEHALF (CODE CIV. PROC., 2025.230) ON THE FOLLOWING CATEGORIES OF INFORMATION LISTED IN PLAINTIFF'S APRIL 2019 SECOND AMENDED DEPOSITION NOTICE, SUBJECT TO THE REVISED DEFINITIONS OF ENGINE DEFECTS AND ELECTRICAL DEFECTS AS SET FORTH IN THE PARTIES' JOINT SEPARATE STATEMENT: CATEGORIES 7, 8, 15, AND 16: GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S BOILERPLATE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. CATEGORIES 12 AND 20: DENIED. THESE CATEGORIES ARE OVERBROAD. CATEGORY 13: GRANTED TO THE EXTENT IT SEEKS INFORMATION REGARDING AVAILABLE FIXES FOR ENGINE DEFECTS IN VEHICLES OF THE SAME MODEL, MAKE, AND YEAR AS THE VEHICLE AT ISSUE. CATEGORY 21: GRANTED TO THE EXTENT IT SEEKS INFORMATION REGARDING AVAILABLE FIXES FOR ELECTRICAL DEFECTS IN VEHICLES OF THE SAME MODEL, MAKE, AND YEAR AS THE VEHICLE AT ISSUE. CATEGORY 22: GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S BOILERPLATE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. CATEGORY 23, 24, 25: GRANTED AS TO THE YEAR 2018, WHEN PLAINTIFF ALLEGEDLY DEMANDED REPURCHASE OF THE VEHICLE. CATEGORIES 28 AND 29: GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S BOILERPLATE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 DIRECTLY BY EMAIL TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT ADRIENNELEIGHT@HOTMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: ADRIENNE ROGERS, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: NANCY PRESANT, CSR 	906, 510-757-8550, REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Further Hearing On Plaintiff Ronald Van Siegman'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Qualified/Person Most Knowledgeable (Specially Set At 1:30 Pm Before Judge Pro Tem Adrienne Rogers)
Off Calendar Aug-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Marcy Sanchez, Esq. 310-929-4900 of Strategic Legal Practices for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Nejla Nassirian, Esq. 916-780-4050 of Universal & Shannon LLP for Defendant through Court Call.
Court reporter: Nancy Presant, CSR 906, 510-757-8550, Reported.

Both sides stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings rules as follows:

Further Hearing On Plaintiff Ronald Van Siegman'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Qualified/Person Most Knowledgeable (Specially Set At 1:30 Pm Before Judge Pro Tem Adrienne Rogers)

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. In June 2019, plaintiff moved to compel the deposition of defendant's PMQ on 39 categories of information. On July 25, the court ordered the parties to meet and confer and reserved jurisdiction on the merits. The parties met and conferred; during that process, plaintiff withdrew numerous categories and revised the definition of several terms, including "Engine Defects" and "Electrical Defects." On August 13, the parties submitted a joint separate statement on the remaining contested categories: 7, 8, 12, 13, 15, 16, 20-25, 28, and 29. Having considered the moving and opposition papers, and the joint separate statement, the court rules follows:

Defendant must produce a person most qualified to testify on its behalf (Code Civ. Proc., 2025.230) on the following categories of information listed in plaintiff's April 2019 second amended deposition notice, subject to the revised definitions of Engine Defects and Electrical Defects as set forth in the parties' joint separate statement:

Categories 7, 8, 15, and 16: GRANTED. Defendant's boilerplate objections are overruled. Categories 12 and 20: DENIED. These categories are overbroad. Category 13: GRANTED to the extent it seeks information regarding available fixes for Engine Defects in vehicles of the same model, make, and year as the vehicle at issue. Category 21: GRANTED to the extent it seeks information regarding available fixes for Electrical Defects in vehicles of the same model, make, and year as the vehicle at issue. Category 22: GRANTED. Defendant's boilerplate objections are overruled. Category 23, 24, 25: GRANTED as to the year 2018, when plaintiff allegedly demanded repurchase of the vehicle. Categories 28 and 29: GRANTED. Defendant's boilerplate objections are overruled. No monetary sanctions.

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 directly by email to the Judge Pro Tem at adrienneleight@hotmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Adrienne Rogers, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Nancy Presant, CSR 906, 510-757-8550, Reported. =(302/JPT)
